tips.title: Front-load context, don't follow up
tips.short: Edit your prompt instead of replying — avoids re-reading the entire conversation history
tips.detail: Each follow-up re-reads the full conversation. A 10-message thread with 500-word responses means 5000+ words of history re-processed per reply. Edit the original message for a clean context reset.

tips.title: Use Projects for persistent context
tips.short: Put repeated background info in CLAUDE.md instead of re-explaining each session
tips.detail: If you repeatedly paste the same background (language, codebase structure, tone), put it in CLAUDE.md or a Project system prompt. Saves tokens every session.

tips.title: Ask for skeletons, not full drafts
tips.short: Request an outline first, approve it, then flesh out — one bad draft costs 4x an outline iteration
tips.detail: For long documents or large features, get the structure right before generating full content. Iterating on an outline is dramatically cheaper than regenerating full drafts.

tips.title: Be surgical with edits
tips.short: Paste only the broken function, not the entire 500-line file
tips.detail: Claude doesn't need the whole file to fix one method. Targeted context = faster responses + fewer tokens. Use Read with line ranges instead of full files.

tips.title: Skip the pleasantries
tips.short: Start with the actual ask — every token of fluff is a token not spent on your task
tips.detail: Remove 'Could you perhaps help me with something if you don't mind?' — start with the task. Claude doesn't need social niceties, and they cost tokens.

tips.title: Specify output length explicitly
tips.short: Add 'respond in under 200 words' or 'bullet points only' — Claude's default is generous
tips.detail: Without length constraints, Claude generates longer responses than needed. Explicit length saves output tokens and keeps context lean for follow-ups.

tips.title: Batch your tasks
tips.short: One message with three tasks beats three separate conversations
tips.detail: 'Do X. Then Y. Then Z.' in one message is dramatically cheaper than three round-trips. Each new conversation has setup overhead.

tips.title: Use Haiku for simple tasks
tips.short: Summarizing, classifying, or quick rewrites don't need Opus — save heavy models for heavy lifting
tips.detail: Via API or subagents, route simple tasks (summarization, classification, reformatting) to Haiku. Reserve Sonnet/Opus for multi-file edits, architecture, and complex reasoning.

tips.title: Don't ask Claude to search its own outputs
tips.short: Scroll up and Cmd+F instead of asking 'what did you say about X?' — saves a full exchange
tips.detail: Asking Claude to recall its own prior output wastes a full round-trip. The text is right there in your conversation — use your editor's search.

signal.query_pattern: what did you say|you mentioned|earlier you

tips.title: Start fresh chats for new topics
tips.short: Long conversations with topic drift force Claude to re-read all unrelated context every reply
tips.detail: Dragging unrelated tasks into a long conversation means Claude re-reads ALL that context every reply. Fresh chat = clean slate = faster + cheaper.
